title: Rosa × laevigata
intro: Rosa × laevigata, also known as the Chinese rose or smooth rose, is a hybrid plant species of the genus Rosa, native to China and Southeast Asia. The leaves and flowers of this plant have been used in traditional Chinese medicine for their health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, and are said to help treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Rosa × laevigata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Rosa × laevigata include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Rosa × laevigata is classified as a hybrid tea rose. This classification is due to its ancestry from the Rosa laevigata and the Rosa species. The combination of its parents' characteristics results in a unique set of traits. As a hybrid, Rosa × laevigata is not a naturally occurring species found in the wild. Its classification as a hybrid tea rose reflects its complex genetic makeup.
uses: Rosa × laevigata, also known as the Chinese rose or yellow rose, has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties. Its flowers, leaves, and roots are used in various herbal remedies to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce pain and improve overall health. In addition, Rosa × laevigata has been used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Rosa × laevigata for medicinal purposes.

properties: Rosa × laevigata, also known as the Chinese climbing rose, has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its various therapeutic properties. Its leaves, flowers, and stems are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating conditions such as fever and respiratory issues. The plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids are thought to contribute to its therapeutic properties, including antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. side_effects: Rosa × laevigata, a species of rose, is often used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits. However, there is limited information available on its side effects in humans.
